revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Iinozaka Kofun Cluster]][[ja:飯野坂古墳群]]
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Iinozaka Kofun Cluster | native_name = 飯野坂古墳群 | native_name_lang = | alternate_name = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | map_type = Japan Miyagi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | map_caption =Iinozaka Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | relief = yes | coordinates ={{coord|38|09|44|N|140|52|38|E||display=inline}}; {{Coord|38.16222222|N|140.87722222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| location ={{ill|Natori, Miyagi|en|Natori, Miyagi|qid=Q642018|lt=Natori, Miyagi|1=Natori, Miyagi}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}; {{ill|Natori|qid=Q642018|1=Natori, Miyagi}}| region = {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|qid=Q129465|lt=Tōhoku region|1=Tōhoku region}} | type = | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|qid=Q30834580|1=Historic Site of Japan}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Iinozaka kofun group'''|飯野坂古墳群|Iinozaka kofun-gun}} is cluster of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|tumuli]] located in what is now the city of {{ill|Natori, Miyagi|en|Natori, Miyagi|qid=Q642018|lt=Natori|1=Natori, Miyagi}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|en|Miyagi Prefecture|qid=Q47896|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|1=Miyagi Prefecture}} in the {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|qid=Q129465|lt=Tōhoku region|1=Tōhoku region}} of northern {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The presence of an unusual number of large keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' located so close together drew attention, and the site received protection by the central government as a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1976.<ref name= "Bunka">{{cite web|url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162081|title=飯野坂古墳群|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 December 2016}}</ref>
==Overview== The site is located on the northern side of 30-meter high hill located approximately 2 kilometers south of {{ill|Natori Station|en|Natori Station|qid=Q841593|lt=Natori Station|1=Natori Station}} in the Natori Plains. The site consists of five large "two conjoined rectangle" type ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}), and two {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}})-style ''kofun'':
{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=''Zenpō-kōhō-fun''|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} * Kannonzuka kofun (観音塚古墳), length 63 meters; rear portion: 30 meters wide, 6 meters high, front portion: 23 meters wide, 5 meters high * Miyayama kofun (宮山古墳), length 70 meters; rear portion: 35 meters wide, 5.5 meters high, front portion: 25 meters wide, 3.5 meters high *Yakushido kofun (薬師堂古墳), total length 68 meters; rear portion: 35 meters wide, 6.4 meters high, front portion: 21 meters wide, 3.6 meters high *Yamakyo kofun (山居古墳), length 60 meters; rear portion: 30 meters wide, 5.3 meters high, front portion: 30 meters wide, 3.5 meters high *Yamakyo-kita kofun (山居北古墳), length 42 meter; rear portion: 20 meters wide, 3 meters high, front portion: 12 meters wide, 1.3 meters high
''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}'' *Kannonzuka-kita No.1, 14 meters x 14 meters *Kannonzuka-kita No.2, 14 meters x 14 meters
The site was surveyed by the Natori Board of Education in 1975 and 1976, although no excavations have been conducted. From the shape and construction of the mounds and from pot-shaped {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}} which have been recovered, the tombs are believed to date from the 4th century AD. The site was endangered by the expansion of nearby housing developments in the early 1970s., but the presence of so many ''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}'' in one location is unique, and the site received protection as a National Historic Site,
It is about a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Natori Station|en|Natori Station|qid=Q841593|lt=Natori Station|1=Natori Station}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|qid=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|en|Tōhoku Main Line|qid=Q681190|lt=Tōhoku Main Line|1=Tōhoku Main Line}}. Although the site is open to the public, there are no facilities and some of the ''kofun'' are covered with trees and dense undergrowth.
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyagi)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyagi)|qid=Q6573276|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyagi)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyagi)}} *[[Raijinyama Kofun]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.pref.miyagi.jp/site/sitei/kuni-siseki22.html Miyagi Prefecture official site] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.city.natori.miyagi.jp/soshiki/kyouiku/node_28152/node_1784/node_1461/node_1639 Natori city official site] {{in lang|ja}} ]] {{Kofun navbar}}
